title: Hot Garlic Chicken
time: Servings: 4 portions | Time: Prep: 20 mins | Cooking: 15 mins

ingredients.title: For Chicken
items: 500 g boneless chicken breast or thigh, cut into bite-sized cubes

ingredients.title: For Sauce
items: 3 tbsp oil (neutral, e.g., peanut or canola)
3–4 dried red chilies, broken (optional: soaked in warm water for softer texture)
1 medium onion, diced into large cubes
1 medium red bell pepper, diced into large cubes
2 tbsp chili paste (Sichuan or homemade chili-garlic paste)
2 tsp cornstarch mixed with 3 tbsp water (slurry)

instruction.title: Marinate & Prep Chicken
steps: In a bowl, mix chicken cubes with light soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, ginger-garlic paste, white pepper, and cornstarch.

instruction.title: Fry Chicken
steps: Heat a wok with 2 tbsp oil until smoking hot.
Sear chicken pieces on high heat until golden and 80% cooked. Remove and set aside.

instruction.title: Build Hot Garlic Sauce
steps: In the same wok, add 1 tbsp oil.
Stir-fry chopped garlic until fragrant but not burnt.
Add dried red chilies, chopped ginger, fresh chilies, onion, and bell pepper. Toss on high heat for 30–40 seconds (should remain crunchy).
Stir in chili paste, ketchup, dark soy, light soy, vinegar, and sugar. Cook until the sauce bubbles.
Add chicken stock and bring to a simmer.

instruction.title: Thicken & Finish
steps: Return chicken to wok, toss well.
Stir in cornstarch slurry gradually to get a glossy, thick sauce that coats the chicken.
Adjust seasoning with salt and a touch of sesame oil for aroma.

instruction.title: Garnish & Serve
steps: Sprinkle spring onions (and sesame seeds if using).
Serve piping hot with fried rice, noodles, or steamed jasmine rice.

chefTips: High heat wok cooking is essential for the smoky "wok hei" flavor.
Balance spicy, tangy, sweet, and umami elements carefully.
Use Shaoxing wine for authentic depth of flavor.
Sauce should be thick, glossy, and cling to chicken, not watery.
